name: ◁ Lahm Bi AjeenSPICED GROUND BEEF FLATBREAD
servings: prep time: 20 minutes, plus 1 hour resting

ingredients.item: 1 cup (240 ml) lukewarm water
ingredients.original: 1 cup (240 ml) lukewarm water

ingredients.item: 2½ cups (300 g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
ingredients.original: 2½ cups (300 g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon kosher salt
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon kosher salt

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) olive oil, plus more for greasing
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) olive oil, plus more for greasing

ingredients.item: 1 pound (454 g) lean ground beef
ingredients.original: 1 pound (454 g) lean ground beef

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) tomato paste
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) tomato paste

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 ml) tomato passata (puree)
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 ml) tomato passata (puree)

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon pomegranate molasses
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon pomegranate molasses

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon black pepper

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon kosher salt
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon kosher salt

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon ground allspice
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon ground allspice

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon sumac
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon sumac

ingredients.item: ½ cup (25 g) roughly chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.original: ½ cup (25 g) roughly chopped fresh parsley

ingredients.item: 1 TO MAKE THE DOUGH: Place the yeast and sugar in a small bowl with the lukewarm water and mix with a spoon. Let stand for 2 to 3 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 TO MAKE THE DOUGH: Place the yeast and sugar in a small bowl with the lukewarm water and mix with a spoon. Let stand for 2 to 3 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 Using a stand mixer with a dough hook, place the flour and salt in the mixing bowl and start mixing on low speed. Add the oil to the bowl and continue to mix until the oil is fully incorporated into the flour. Gradually add the water-yeast mixture and continue to mix on medium speed. Once the water is fully incorporated, the dough will start to form. Continue kneading for at least 5 to 7 minutes. The dough is ready when it feels moist but not too sticky and starts to pull away from the bowl. If it feels dry, add more water; if it feels too moist, add a dusting of flour.
ingredients.original: 2 Using a stand mixer with a dough hook, place the flour and salt in the mixing bowl and start mixing on low speed. Add the oil to the bowl and continue to mix until the oil is fully incorporated into the flour. Gradually add the water-yeast mixture and continue to mix on medium speed. Once the water is fully incorporated, the dough will start to form. Continue kneading for at least 5 to 7 minutes. The dough is ready when it feels moist but not too sticky and starts to pull away from the bowl. If it feels dry, add more water; if it feels too moist, add a dusting of flour.

ingredients.item: 3 Rub your hands with some oil and bring the dough together into a ball. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rise for 1 hour.
ingredients.original: 3 Rub your hands with some oil and bring the dough together into a ball. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rise for 1 hour.

ingredients.item: 4 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE FILLING: In a large bowl, combine the onion, parsley, ground beef, tomato paste, passata, pomegranate molasses, pepper, kosher salt, curry powder, allspice, and cinnamon. Mix well using your hands until the ingredients are incorporated.
ingredients.original: 4 MEANWHILE, MAKE THE FILLING: In a large bowl, combine the onion, parsley, ground beef, tomato paste, passata, pomegranate molasses, pepper, kosher salt, curry powder, allspice, and cinnamon. Mix well using your hands until the ingredients are incorporated.

ingredients.item: 5 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C) on the bake setting. If you have one, place a pizza stone on the bottom rack to heat up; otherwise, line a large sheet pan with parchment paper (no need to preheat the sheet pan).
ingredients.original: 5 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C) on the bake setting. If you have one, place a pizza stone on the bottom rack to heat up; otherwise, line a large sheet pan with parchment paper (no need to preheat the sheet pan).

ingredients.item: 6 Once the dough has risen, cut it into 6 equal-size pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
ingredients.original: 6 Once the dough has risen, cut it into 6 equal-size pieces and roll each piece into a ball.

ingredients.item: 7 Lightly sprinkle some flour on your work surface and, using a rolling pin, roll out one of the dough balls into a thin circle (the size does not matter and can be to your preference). Gently remove the dough from the work surface and place it either on a pizza peel lined with parchment paper, if you are using a pizza stone, or on the parchment-lined sheet pan. Place 3 or 4 spoonfuls of the filling on the rolled-out dough, smoothing it out using a spoon to cover the surface with a thin layer, leaving roughly a ¼-inch (6 mm) border.
ingredients.original: 7 Lightly sprinkle some flour on your work surface and, using a rolling pin, roll out one of the dough balls into a thin circle (the size does not matter and can be to your preference). Gently remove the dough from the work surface and place it either on a pizza peel lined with parchment paper, if you are using a pizza stone, or on the parchment-lined sheet pan. Place 3 or 4 spoonfuls of the filling on the rolled-out dough, smoothing it out using a spoon to cover the surface with a thin layer, leaving roughly a ¼-inch (6 mm) border.
